items.title: The LA Clippers are 1-1 in Summer League
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The LA Clippers are 1-1 in Summer League

Final score: Clippers 104, Jazz 82

Nick Martinelli: 14 points, 7/11 FGs, 6 rebounds, 1 steal

FGs: LAC 35/81, 43.%; UTA 27/63, 42.9%

Big possession battle game for Clippers: 15-8 offensive rebound advantage, 14-11 opp. turnover advantage

items.title: (no text)
items.author: Hoop Informatics

items.title: Final: Clippers 104, Jazz 82. Keaton Wagler’s 3rd quarter display lifted Los Angeles to their first Summer League win as the guard scored 18 in the frame and 23 for the game. Baba Miller had 15 points, 5 rebounds; Sean Pedulla was +30; Nick Martinelli added 14 points, 6 rebounds.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Final: Clippers 104, Jazz 82. Keaton Wagler’s 3rd quarter display lifted Los Angeles to their first Summer League win as the guard scored 18 in the frame and 23 for the game. Baba Miller had 15 points, 5 rebounds; Sean Pedulla was +30; Nick Martinelli added 14 points, 6 rebounds.

items.title: Keaton Wagler’s night is done after a 23-point performance, with 18 coming in the third quarter. Wagler, who played 28 minutes, finishes with 4 assists, as well. The No. 5 overall selection was 6-for-16 from the field but went 4-for-7 on 3s. A very good display of aggression.
items.author: Justin Russo

items.title: Fever return to Las Vegas and leave with their second win over Aces in a week, 109-75. They've now 4 of their last 5 games.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever return to Las Vegas and leave with their second win over Aces in a week, 109-75. They've now 4 of their last 5 games.

Boston (19/11) was splendid, Mitchell scored 27, Cunningham had 20, and Clark added 12-7-6.

Next: Wed. v GSV

items.title: Fever crush the Aces in their best performance of the season. A 34-point road win to close out a 3-1 road trip and improve to 14-9. Final score: 109-75. All of Caitlin Clark, Kelsey Mitchell, and Aliyah Boston were excellent. Sophie Cunningham couldn't miss.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Fever crush the Aces in their best performance of the season. A 34-point road win to close out a 3-1 road trip and improve to 14-9. Final score: 109-75. All of Caitlin Clark, Kelsey Mitchell, and Aliyah Boston were excellent. Sophie Cunningham couldn't miss.

Clippers outscoring Jazz 10-6 off turnovers

items.title: Halftime: Clippers 40, Jazz 38. It’s been a more aggressive offensive showing for Keaton Wagler tonight, as the No. 5 overall pick has already taken 7 shots. Wagler has 5 points and 3 assists. Baba Miller with 9 points. Darryn Peterson leads all scorers with 12 points for Utah.

Clark (5/4a) played two stints of 5.5mins.

items.title: Aliyah Boston just went back to the locker room with about 25 seconds left in the half — before the rest of the Fever. Worth monitoring. She, and the Fever generally, played a great half. it's 59-48 Fever at the break.

items.title: Will Riley finishes with 32 points on 96% true shooting in 28 minutes in a Wizards win vs the Kings.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Will Riley finishes with 32 points on 96% true shooting in 28 minutes in a Wizards win vs the Kings. 

Screenshot via hoopsjunkie.io which has live summer league advanced stats

images.alt: Will Riley (Washington Wizards) vs. Sacramento Kings, July 12, 2026 — Wizards leading 102-85 late in Q4. Riley's line: 28 minutes, 32 points, 6 rebounds, 3 assists, 0 steals, 0 blocks. Shooting: 9/14 FG (64.3%), 6/8 3PT (75.0%), 5/6 FT (83.3%), 96.2% TS. Additional stats: 26.9 game score, +21 plus/minus, 2 turnovers, 3 fouls, 2 offensive rebounds, 4 defensive rebounds.

items.title: Tyler Marsh said the Sky's recurring late-game issue has been an inability to put together timely stops and timely scores in the same stretch.
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
Tyler Marsh said the Sky's recurring late-game issue has been an inability to put together timely stops and timely scores in the same stretch. 

“We've got a locker room and coaching staff that's tired of being on this end of it."

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg again got more bragging rights against a former Michigan teammate
items.author: Dalton Johnson
items.body_excerpt:
Yaxel Lendeborg again got more bragging rights against a former Michigan teammate 

His 3-point shot remains hot, and his defense remains an afterthought

media.title: Warriors' Yaxel Lendeborg outshines Michigan teammate Aday Mara, but room for improvement is evident
media.description: Golden State Warriors rookie Yaxel Lendeborg was the leading scorer in Sunday's summer league win, but the No. 11 pick was a mixed bag on defense.
